verses.text: When thou art come in unto the land the LORD thy God giveth thee, take of the first of all the fruit of the earth, put it in a basket, go unto the place which the LORD shall choose. Say unto the priest: I profess this day unto the LORD that I am come unto the country which He sware unto our fathers. The priest shall take the basket, set it before the altar. Guidance: A small basket and a big sentence. The first fruits of the harvest go up, and the speech that goes with them locates the family inside God's promise. Profession — saying out loud what you believe — is part of the offering. Faith without words is half a gift.

Thou shalt speak before the LORD thy God: A Syrian ready to perish was my father; he went down into Egypt, sojourned there, became a nation great. The Egyptians afflicted us, laid hard bondage. We cried unto the LORD; He heard, looked on our affliction; brought us forth with a mighty hand, a stretched out arm, into this land flowing with milk and honey. Now, behold, I have brought the firstfruits which Thou hast given me. Guidance: Israel's earliest creed, recited at every firstfruit basket. 'A wandering Aramean was my father' — the worshipper inserts himself into a story older than his birth. The harvest in his hand is not his achievement; it is the latest chapter of a long rescue.

Thou shalt rejoice in every good thing which the LORD thy God hath given unto thee, thy house, thou, the Levite, the stranger that is among you. Guidance: One verse, four guest list categories. The harvest table includes the family, the priest, the foreigner. Joy is what God wanted to come out of the basket — and joy that shares is the only joy that lasts.

When thou hast made an end of tithing thy increase the third year, given unto the Levite, stranger, fatherless, widow, that they may eat within thy gates and be filled; then thou shalt say before the LORD: I have brought away the hallowed things, given them according to all Thy commandments; I have not transgressed. I have hearkened to the voice of the LORD my God. Look down from Thy holy habitation, bless Thy people Israel. Guidance: A liturgy of clean conscience. The triennial tithe came with an honest declaration — did you give what you owed and not pinch the holy for personal use? The prayer ends with a tender ask: look down and bless. He gives obedience; He asks blessing.

This day the LORD thy God hath commanded thee to do these statutes and judgments: thou shalt keep and do them with all thine heart, and with all thy soul. Thou hast avouched the LORD this day to be thy God; the LORD hath avouched thee this day to be His peculiar people, as He hath promised thee; to make thee high above all nations in praise, in name, in honour; that thou mayest be an holy people unto the LORD thy God. Guidance: Two avouchments, sealed in one day. You said: He is my God. He said: They are my people. The covenant is mutual public testimony — both parties on record. Holiness is the destination of belonging.
